description: A CAN node repeatedly errors and eventually goes bus-off because its bit timing or sample point disagrees with the rest of the bus.
body: Failure mode: one node's bit-timing segments place its sample point differently from peers, so it misreads bits near edges and floods the bus with error frames until the controller disables itself. Detection signals: a rising transmit-error counter, error frames clustered around bit boundaries, and a node entering bus-off after a burst. Do not keep retransmitting while the error counter climbs. Safer fallback: recompute the bit-timing segments for the shared bitrate, align the sample point across all nodes, and reset the controller out of bus-off.
